summary_en: Tender offer by Cultura Convenience Club for Jimoty shares at 1,420 yen/share targeting 66.67% minimum acceptance (6,560,000 shares) to effect delisting and squeeze-out. Board recommends participation. Major shareholders (Kato 10.08%, NTT DoCoMo 18.76%, Proto Corporation 12.39%) have committed to tender 41.51% aggregate. Correction to prior May 15 disclosure regarding pledge mechanics on founder's shares.
title_en: (Correction) Notice of Correction to Board Recommendation and Tender Offer Solicitation Regarding Cultura Convenience Club's Public Tender Offer for Jimoty Shares
translation_en_brief:
On May 26, 2026, Jimoty disclosed a correction to its May 15 board recommendation notice regarding Cultura Convenience Club's public tender offer for Jimoty shares. The tender offer price is fixed at 1,420 yen per share. The offeror has set a minimum acceptance threshold of 6,560,000 shares (66.67% of outstanding shares), calculated to ensure a two-thirds supermajority for post-delisting shareholder votes on consolidation. No maximum threshold applies; all tendered shares above the minimum will be purchased.

Three major shareholders have committed to tender: founder and CEO Takahiro Kato (10.08% ownership, though 7.22% are pledged to Daiwa Securities and subject to pledge release by offer period close), NTT DoCoMo (18.76%), and Proto Corporation (12.39%). Combined, committed tenders represent 41.51% of shares outstanding (including 27,200 shares underlying Kato's warrants).

The corrected disclosure addresses mechanics of Kato's pledged shares and his cooperation agreement in the squeeze-out process post-delisting. Kato will participate in tendering unless pledge release proves impractical, triggering a corresponding reduction in the minimum threshold. The tender offer is designed to effect complete privatization of Jimoty through subsequent share consolidation, requiring the two-thirds approval that the minimum threshold secures.
